title: Frame embeddings and search index
analysis: Implement autopilot/analyze/embeddings.py with four functions: compute_embeddings (SigLIP 2 vision encoder at 0.5 FPS, stores 768-d float32 BLOBs in clip_embeddings table), build_search_index (FAISS IVF), search_by_text (SigLIP text encoder + FAISS), and search_by_image (SigLIP vision encoder + FAISS). The codebase follows a consistent pattern across analyze modules (asr.py, objects.py, faces.py): lazy imports of heavy ML libs inside functions, TYPE_CHECKING guards for type hints, GPUScheduler context manager for model access, CatalogDB for storage with transactional context managers, custom error classes, structured logging, and idempotency checks. Tests use unittest.mock with sys.modules patching for heavy dependencies (cv2, transformers, etc.) and the catalog_db fixture from conftest.py. The DB already has clip_embeddings table (media_id, frame_number, embedding BLOB) with batch_insert_embeddings and get_embeddings_for_media methods. A new get_all_clip_embeddings() DB method is needed for build_search_index (follows get_all_face_embeddings pattern). The config already has clip_model='google/siglip2-so400m-patch14'. FAISS index needs a sidecar JSON mapping file to map vector indices back to (media_id, frame_number) tuples for search result resolution.

prerequisites.description: Add get_all_clip_embeddings() method to CatalogDB that returns all rows from clip_embeddings with non-null embedding (media_id, frame_number, embedding BLOB). Pattern follows get_all_face_embeddings(). Write tests for it in test_embeddings.py::TestDBHelpers: test empty table returns [], test with inserted rows returns all, test multiple media IDs returned correctly.

steps.description: Write TestPublicAPI class in test_embeddings.py: (1) test_embedding_error_importable - EmbeddingError importable and is Exception subclass; (2) test_compute_embeddings_signature - importable with positional params (media_id, video_path, db, scheduler, config) and keyword-only batch_size; (3) test_build_search_index_signature - importable with params (db, output_path); (4) test_search_by_text_signature - importable with params (query, index_path, model, top_k=10); (5) test_search_by_image_signature - importable with params (image, index_path, model, top_k=10). Verify all are in __all__.

Create autopilot/analyze/embeddings.py with module skeleton: docstring, TYPE_CHECKING imports for GPUScheduler/ModelConfig/CatalogDB, EmbeddingError class, module-level logger, __all__ exports, function stubs with correct signatures. compute_embeddings and build_search_index raise NotImplementedError. search_by_text/search_by_image return empty lists. Follow objects.py module pattern.

Write TestComputeSampleIndices class for _compute_sample_indices helper: (1) test_half_fps_30fps - 300 frames at 30fps -> [0, 60, 120, 180, 240]; (2) test_half_fps_24fps - 240 frames at 24fps -> [0, 48, 96, 144, 192]; (3) test_empty_video - 0 frames -> []; (4) test_short_video - 30 frames at 30fps -> [0]; (5) test_low_fps - 10 frames at 1fps -> [0, 2, 4, 6, 8].
Implement _compute_sample_indices(total_frames, fps, sample_fps=0.5) helper: interval = max(1, int(fps / sample_fps)), return list(range(0, total_frames, interval)). Handle total_frames <= 0 -> [].

Write TestIdempotency class: (1) test_existing_embeddings_skips - insert media + clip embeddings into DB, call compute_embeddings, verify scheduler.model not called; (2) test_no_existing_embeddings_proceeds - insert media only, call compute_embeddings with full mocks (cv2, model, scheduler), verify scheduler.model IS called. Follow pattern from test_objects.py::TestIdempotency.

Implement compute_embeddings idempotency check: call db.get_embeddings_for_media(media_id), return early if non-empty with INFO log. Add video path validation (path.exists() check, cap.isOpened check), lazy cv2 import. Enough to pass idempotency and basic structure tests.

Write TestErrorHandling class: (1) test_video_not_found_raises - nonexistent path raises EmbeddingError matching 'not found'; (2) test_video_not_opened_raises - cap.isOpened() False raises EmbeddingError matching 'Failed to open'; (3) test_frame_read_failure_skipped - one frame read fails, other frames still produce embeddings in DB.

Implement compute_embeddings error handling: path.exists() check before cv2 import raising EmbeddingError, cap.isOpened() check after VideoCapture raising EmbeddingError, frame read failure skip with warning log. Follow objects.py/faces.py error handling pattern.

Write TestEmbeddingComputation class: (1) test_correct_embedding_count - 300 frames at 30fps stores 5 embeddings (0.5 FPS); (2) test_embedding_size_768d - each stored BLOB is 3072 bytes (768 * 4 bytes float32); (3) test_embedding_roundtrip - stored embedding recoverable via np.frombuffer dtype=float32 matches original; (4) test_model_called_per_frame - model encoder called once per sampled frame; (5) test_correct_frame_numbers - DB rows have frame_numbers matching sample indices [0, 60, 120, 180, 240]. Mock SigLIP model to return deterministic 768-d embeddings, mock cv2.VideoCapture.

Implement compute_embeddings core loop: load SigLIP (model, processor) tuple via scheduler.model(config.clip_model), iterate sampled frames, read frame with cv2, convert BGR->RGB via PIL.Image, preprocess via processor(images=image, return_tensors='pt'), encode via model.get_image_features(), L2-normalize output, pack 768-d float32 to bytes, accumulate rows, batch_insert_embeddings in with-db block. Release cap in finally block.

Write TestLogging class: (1) test_log_contains_media_id_on_start - INFO log includes media_id and frame info; (2) test_log_contains_completion - log includes 'Completed' and embedding count; (3) test_log_skipping_on_idempotent - log mentions 'skipping' on idempotent skip; (4) test_log_contains_fps - log mentions fps info.

Add structured logging to compute_embeddings: start message with media_id/total_frames/fps, completion message with embedding count, skip message on idempotent early return. Pattern: logger = logging.getLogger(__name__).

Write TestBuildSearchIndex class: (1) test_empty_db_no_index - empty DB logs and returns, no files written; (2) test_small_dataset_creates_index - insert 10 mock 768-d embeddings, build index, output_path file exists; (3) test_id_mapping_written - sidecar .ids.json exists with correct [[media_id, frame_number], ...] entries matching inserted data; (4) test_index_vector_count - FAISS index ntotal matches embedding count; (5) test_index_dimension - FAISS index d equals 768. Use faiss-cpu for real index behavior in tests.

Implement build_search_index: load embeddings via db.get_all_clip_embeddings(), stack into float32 numpy matrix, L2-normalize. Choose index type: IndexFlatIP for n<256, IndexIVFFlat with nlist=max(1,int(sqrt(n))) for n>=256 (train on data, then add). Write index via faiss.write_index(). Write sidecar .ids.json with list of [media_id, frame_number] pairs. Handle empty DB with early return and log.

Write TestSearchByText class: (1) test_returns_correct_types - returns list of (str, int, float) tuples; (2) test_top_k_limits_results - top_k=3 returns at most 3 results; (3) test_text_encoder_called - model text features method invoked with query string; (4) test_results_contain_valid_media_ids - all media_ids in results are from the indexed data.

Implement search_by_text: lazy import faiss. Load FAISS index via faiss.read_index(str(index_path)). Load .ids.json mapping. Extract (model, processor) from model param. Encode text: processor(text=[query], return_tensors='pt', padding=True) -> model.get_text_features() -> L2-normalize. Search index.search(query_vec, top_k). Map FAISS indices to (media_id, frame_number, distance) via mapping. Return results list.

Write TestSearchByImage class: (1) test_returns_correct_types - returns list of (str, int, float) tuples; (2) test_top_k_limits_results - top_k=3 returns at most 3; (3) test_image_encoder_called - model vision features method invoked with image; (4) test_results_contain_valid_frame_numbers - all frame_numbers in results are from indexed data.

Implement search_by_image: load FAISS index + mapping. Extract (model, processor) from model param. Convert np.ndarray image to PIL, encode via processor(images=image, return_tensors='pt') -> model.get_image_features() -> L2-normalize. Search index. Map results. Return tuples. Extract shared _load_index_and_mapping helper used by both search functions.

Write TestIntegration class: test_compute_build_search_pipeline - (1) compute_embeddings on mock video with 5 sampled frames, verify 5 rows in DB; (2) build_search_index from DB, verify index file + mapping exist with 5 entries; (3) search_by_text returns results with valid media_id; (4) search_by_image returns results; (5) all frame_numbers in results are from the original sample indices.
Final verification pass: run full test suite, fix any edge cases. Ensure cap.release() in finally block, proper error wrapping, clean docstrings and type hints. Verify __all__ exports are correct.

design_decisions.decision: Use inner product (IndexFlatIP) for FAISS similarity since SigLIP embeddings are L2-normalized
design_decisions.rationale: For L2-normalized vectors, inner product equals cosine similarity and is faster than L2 distance. SigLIP produces normalized embeddings, so IP gives correct semantic similarity ranking. Higher IP score = more similar.

design_decisions.decision: Store FAISS ID mapping as a JSON sidecar file (.ids.json) alongside the index
design_decisions.rationale: FAISS indices only store integer IDs. A JSON file mapping FAISS row index -> [media_id, frame_number] is simple, human-readable, and avoids pickle security concerns. The mapping list is small (~90K entries for 50h at 0.5 FPS = ~3.6MB JSON).

design_decisions.decision: Two-tier FAISS index strategy: IndexFlatIP (<256), IndexIVFFlat (>=256)
design_decisions.rationale: Small datasets don't need IVF overhead - exact brute-force search is fast. Medium-to-large datasets benefit from IVF partitioning with nlist=sqrt(n). The PRD example (50h at 0.5 FPS = 90K) falls in the IVFFlat range. IndexIVFPQ can be added later if needed for >1M vectors.

design_decisions.decision: Frame sampling at 0.5 FPS via interval = max(1, int(fps / sample_fps))
design_decisions.rationale: 0.5 FPS = 1 frame every 2 seconds. For 30fps video: interval=60. Using fps/sample_fps is general. max(1,...) prevents zero interval for very low FPS. int() rounds down, slightly over-sampling rather than under-sampling to avoid missing content.

design_decisions.decision: GPU scheduler model entry stores (model, processor) tuple for SigLIP
design_decisions.rationale: SigLIP requires both model and processor for inference. Storing them together in the scheduler means a single model() context manager call provides everything needed. The caller unpacks: with scheduler.model(name) as (model, processor).

design_decisions.decision: search_by_text/search_by_image take pre-loaded model tuple rather than scheduler
design_decisions.rationale: Per task spec, search functions accept a 'model' parameter directly. Search may be called interactively many times - the caller manages model lifecycle. This decouples search from the GPU scheduler's batch processing context.

design_decisions.decision: 768-d embeddings stored as raw float32 BLOBs in SQLite
design_decisions.rationale: 768 * 4 bytes = 3072 bytes per embedding. Consistent with how face embeddings (512-d BLOBs) are stored. numpy tobytes/frombuffer provides zero-copy serialization. SQLite handles BLOBs efficiently.

design_decisions.decision: Add get_all_clip_embeddings() to CatalogDB rather than raw SQL in embeddings.py
design_decisions.rationale: All SQL is encapsulated in CatalogDB methods following the get_all_face_embeddings pattern. Keeps embeddings.py focused on ML logic.

design_decisions.decision: L2-normalize embeddings at compute time before storage
design_decisions.rationale: Pre-normalizing avoids redundant computation during index building and search. FAISS IndexFlatIP on normalized vectors gives cosine similarity directly. All downstream consumers get properly normalized embeddings.

reuse.how: Existing method accepts (media_id, frame_number, embedding_blob) tuples. Use directly in compute_embeddings to store SigLIP embeddings.
Use for idempotency check in compute_embeddings - if media already has embeddings, skip processing.
Table already exists with (media_id, frame_number, embedding BLOB) PK. No schema changes needed.
Reuse in-memory CatalogDB fixture with autocommit for all embedding tests.
Replicate _make_mock_capture() and _make_mock_cv2() patterns for mocking cv2.VideoCapture with configurable fps, total_frames. Use patch.dict(sys.modules, {'cv2': mock_cv2}).
Use scheduler.model(config.clip_model) to load/unload SigLIP model, same as objects.py uses scheduler.model(config.yolo_variant).
Default value 'google/siglip2-so400m-patch14' already configured. Access via config.clip_model in compute_embeddings.

reuse.what: Module structure pattern (docstring, TYPE_CHECKING, __all__, logger, error class)
reuse.how: Follow identical module layout: future annotations import, TYPE_CHECKING guard for GPUScheduler/ModelConfig/CatalogDB, __all__ list, module-level logger, custom error class inheriting Exception, lazy heavy imports.
Model get_all_clip_embeddings() after this: SELECT media_id, frame_number, embedding FROM clip_embeddings WHERE embedding IS NOT NULL.
